message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding per-entry TTL support to cache layers (memory+disk) via configure_cache/Cache/set_ttl

type: INTENT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding ttl param to Cache.__init__/get/put, TTLCache import, set_ttl() helper in clients/__init__.py, ttl param on configure_cache and DSPY_CACHE; will add ttl as new keyword param appended after existing params to minimize conflicts

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Compression feature done: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params (appended at end of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, after existing params), added _serialize_for_disk/_deserialize_from_disk/set_compression methods, changed put() to store self._serialize_for_disk(value) and get() to call self._deserialize_from_disk(raw) on the disk_cache path only; memory_cache path untouched. Should merge cleanly with TTL if you also append ttl at the end of both signatures and only touch get/put in the disk_cache branches - let me know if you need get()/put() bodies coordinated since we both modify those methods

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: TTL feature complete: appended ttl as last param in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache; get() only adds an expire() call in the miss branch, put() only wraps the disk_cache assignment in an if/else for expire=ttl - both are minimal, isolated edits that should merge fine with your serialize/deserialize wrapping
